top_findings.summary: The skill description includes the ability to execute arbitrary shell commands via the `!command` syntax in the command file, which is excessive for the stated primary purpose of identifying underspecified areas in feature specs and asking targeted clarification questions. This unrestricted command execution could allow actions unrelated to spec clarification, such as accessing sensitive system files, performing network operations, or modifying data beyond the project scope, posing a significant security risk without clear justification for the core functionality.
